key_insight: A hybrid phase-field and attention-based deep learning framework can predict 2D and 3D ternary alloy microstructure evolution, combining physical fidelity with fast long-horizon forecasting.
engineering_relevance: This enables more efficient design and optimization of nanoporous and multicomponent materials by reducing the cost of long microstructure simulations.
method: Phase-field model generates early-stage spinodal dealloying dynamics.
Autoencoder compresses 256x256x3 microstructure fields into compact latent features.
Attention-enhanced ConvLSTM and slice-by-slice 3D strategy predict late-stage evolution up to 128x128x128x3 cases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports predictive accuracy maintained up to 400 timesteps ahead and generalization to compositions outside the training distribution.
watch_item: 3D extension via slice-by-slice prediction may be sensitive to inter-slice consistency and to shifts in morphology outside the learned regime.
paper_title: Bridging Phase-Field Model and Deep Learning for Predicting 2D and 3D Microstructure Evolution in Ternary Alloys
